name: OpenFOAM On Slurm
summary: Run OpenFOAM CFD cases on Slurm with decomposition, MPI launch, and reconstruction planning.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm wrapper for OpenFOAM cases, including case staging, domain decomposition, solver launch with -parallel, reconstruction, output records, and a dry-run-first cavity smoke case.

tools.name: blockMesh
tools.notes: OpenFOAM mesh utility used by the tiny cavity smoke case.

tools.name: decomposePar
tools.notes: OpenFOAM domain decomposition utility for parallel runs.

tools.name: reconstructPar
tools.notes: OpenFOAM reconstruction utility for processor outputs.

tools.name: icoFoam
tools.notes: Default transient incompressible solver used by the smoke case; production cases may use another solver.

inputs.name: case_template
inputs.description: OpenFOAM case directory containing 0, constant, and system directories.

inputs.name: solver
inputs.description: OpenFOAM solver executable, defaulting to icoFoam for the smoke case.

inputs.name: ntasks
inputs.description: Total MPI ranks or Slurm tasks used for domain decomposition and parallel launch.

inputs.name: decomposition_shape
inputs.description: OpenFOAM simple decomposition shape, for example (4 1 1).

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Durable output directory for staged cases, processor outputs, reconstructed fields, and launch records.

outputs.name: openfoam_launch_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable Slurm, MPI, case, decomposition, solver, and reconstruction plan.

outputs.name: staged_case
outputs.description: Copied OpenFOAM case directory used for the actual run when RUN_OPENFOAM=1.

outputs.name: processor_outputs
outputs.description: OpenFOAM processor directories generated by the parallel solver run.

outputs.name: reconstructed_fields
outputs.description: Optional reconstructed OpenFOAM fields generated by reconstructPar.

examples.title: Plan or run an OpenFOAM cavity smoke case on Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Run plan-only mode first, then set RUN_OPENFOAM=1 on a short debug partition with a site-approved OpenFOAM module or container.
